_description: Example intake form JSON output — Tier 1 Website. This is the exact structure produced when a candidate completes the 16-step intake form. Use this as a reference for populating the candidate page template. Every key maps to a {{placeholder}} token in the atp_cand_* shortcodes.
ballotpedia_status: Already completed — URL provided in Step 0
homepage_intro: John Stacy is a lifelong Rockwall County resident, Army veteran, and small business owner running to bring fiscal accountability and common-sense leadership to the Commissioner's Court.
bio_full:
John Stacy grew up on a family ranch in eastern Rockwall County. After graduating from Rockwall High School, he enlisted in the U.S. Army and served two tours overseas. He earned his degree in Public Administration from Texas A&M and returned home to build Stacy Construction, which has employed over 200 local workers in the last 15 years.

As a father of three and active member of First Baptist Church, John has served on the Rockwall ISD facilities committee, coached Little League for 8 years, and chaired the county's infrastructure advisory board. He believes government should be run like a well-managed business — transparent budgets, measurable outcomes, and respect for taxpayers.
why_running: Rockwall County is one of the fastest-growing counties in Texas, but our infrastructure hasn't kept pace. Roads are crumbling, development fees aren't covering the true cost of growth, and the current commissioner hasn't held a public town hall in three years. I'm running because taxpayers deserve a commissioner who shows up, listens, and gets results.
differentiator: John is the only candidate with both military discipline and construction industry expertise — he knows how to manage budgets, build infrastructure, and lead teams under pressure.
key_messages:
Message 1: Rockwall County's roads and infrastructure can't keep up with growth — I have a plan to fix that without raising taxes.
Message 2: As a veteran and business owner, I know how to manage budgets and deliver results on time.
Message 3: I'll hold monthly town halls so every resident has a voice in how their tax dollars are spent.
policy_passions: Infrastructure investment, fiscal transparency, veteran services, responsible growth management
issue_positions:
Infrastructure & Transportation: Rockwall County has added 18,000 residents in 5 years but hasn't expanded a single major road. I'll prioritize the FM 552 corridor widening, the Horizon Road interchange, and a county-wide road condition assessment in my first 90 days. We'll use impact fees from new development to fund improvements — growth should pay for itself.

Taxes & Government Spending: I'll publish a quarterly budget dashboard so every taxpayer can see where their money goes. I oppose a property tax increase and will find savings through competitive bidding, contract audits, and eliminating redundant county positions.

Public Safety & Crime: Our sheriff's department is understaffed for a county our size. I'll support hiring 12 additional deputies and expanding the mental health crisis response team. Safe communities attract businesses and keep property values strong.

Veterans & Military: Rockwall County has over 4,000 veterans but no dedicated county veteran services coordinator. I'll create that position and partner with the VA North Texas system to bring mobile health clinics to our community.

Housing & Development: Growth is good — but only if it's managed. I'll update the county's land use plan for the first time in 12 years, require developers to fund infrastructure concurrent with construction, and protect agricultural land from unchecked sprawl.
opponents_missing_issue: No one is talking about the county's unfunded pension liability. We owe $14M in retirement obligations with no plan to close the gap. I'll propose a funded ratio target and annual reporting.
changed_position: I used to oppose impact fees on new development, believing the market would self-correct. After watching our roads deteriorate while developers built 3,000 new homes with no infrastructure contributions, I changed my mind. Growth should pay its fair share.
previous_experience: U.S. Army — Infantry Officer, 2 tours overseas (2003-2009). Rockwall ISD Facilities Committee Chair (2018-2022). Rockwall County Infrastructure Advisory Board Chair (2020-2024). Little League head coach, 8 seasons.
education_1: Texas A&M University, B.S. Public Administration, 2012
design_notes: Use the star from the logo as a recurring element. Want it to feel serious and trustworthy — not flashy.
survey_tier: Tier 1 — Benchmark: Name ID + favorability baseline. One survey.
survey_intro_text: Your voice matters. Take 2 minutes to share what issues matter most to you and your family in Rockwall County.
survey_display: Both dedicated page and homepage section
sms_optin_language: I agree to receive occasional text updates from the Stacy for Commissioner campaign. Msg & data rates may apply. Reply STOP to opt out.
paidfor_text: Paid for by John Stacy for County Commissioner. David Stacy, Treasurer.
service_providers: America Tracking Polls (polling & website), Anedot (donations), Mailchimp (email)
donation_needed: Already have one — just link it
donation_sms_description: Campaign updates and event alerts via text
endorsements:
Sheriff Terry Garrett — 'John Stacy has my full support. He's the kind of leader who shows up and gets the job done.'
Rockwall County Republican Party — Official endorsement, March 2026
Texas Veterans Coalition — 'A proven leader who served his country and now wants to serve his community.'
Dr. Sarah Mitchell, Rockwall ISD Board President — 'John's work on the facilities committee saved taxpayers $2.3 million. Imagine what he'll do on the Commissioner's Court.'
copy_help: We need help writing some copy
referral_source: Referred by Dan at America Tracking Polls
open_notes: We have a campaign kickoff event on May 15 — would love the site live before then. Also interested in the Tier 2 survey upgrade after the primary.
